transcript: Hello, this is the fraud department at Bank of America. We've detected suspicious transactions on your account ending in 4829. A charge of $2,499 was made at an electronics store in Lagos, Nigeria. To block this transaction immediately, I need to verify your identity. Can you please confirm your Social Security number and the PIN for your debit card?
hints: Asks for SSN and PIN — banks never do this
Creates panic with specific fake details
Uses urgency to prevent rational thinking

transcript: Hi, this is Mike from IT. We're seeing some unusual network traffic from your workstation. I need to run a quick remote diagnostic. Can you give me your login credentials so I can check your active sessions? This is time-sensitive — we think there might be malware on your machine.
hints: IT never asks for passwords over the phone
